name: Through the Lers pass to the pond
name_original: Par le col de Lers jusqu'à l'étang
region_intro: Road cycling around Auzat is characterized by its challenging Pyrenean terrain, featuring numerous mountain passes and significant elevation gains. The region, part of the Ariège Pyrenees, offers a diverse network of routes with varied gradients and road conditions. Cyclists can expect a wild and majestic mountainous setting, with many routes providing well-paved surfaces suitable for road biking. Best road cycling routes around Auzat The most popular road cycling route is Through the Lers pass to the pond, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant ascent over the Lers Pass. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Col de Port – Lers Pass loop from L'Artigue, a difficult 59.2 miles (95.3 km) path. This route includes challenging climbs over both the Col de Port and Lers Pass, offering extensive mountain views. Local road cyclists also love the Col de la Crouzette (1245 m) – Col de Saraillé loop from Val-de-Sos, an 86.8 miles (139.8 km) trail leading through multiple mountain passes, often completed in about 8 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Auzat is defined by challenging mountain passes, sustained climbs, and wild Pyrenean scenery. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to difficult, with 40 routes available. The routes in Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 6,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Auzat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Auzat?
A: Auzat offers a diverse network of over 40 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: What makes road cycling in Auzat unique?
A: Road cycling in Auzat is characterized by its challenging Pyrenean terrain, featuring numerous mountain passes and significant elevation gains. The region offers a wild and majestic mountainous setting, with many routes providing well-paved surfaces and stunning panoramic views, such as those found at the summit of Goulier-Neige.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Auzat?
A: Yes, while Auzat is known for its challenging terrain, there are 8 routes classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes still offer the beautiful scenery of the Ariège Pyrenees without the extreme climbs.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Auzat is ideal for challenging rides. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945199431/through-the-lers-pass-to-the-pond" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the Lers pass to the pond&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41144157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Sabart Chapel – Plateau de Beille Climb loop from Vicdessos&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gains and demanding ascents. The climb to Goulier-Neige, starting from Vicdessos, is particularly notable for its sustained steep gradients, regularly reaching 9-10%.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Auzat area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35689523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Crouzette (1245 m) – Col de Saraillé loop from Val-de-Sos&lt;/a&gt; stretch for nearly 140 km, including multiple mountain passes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20533050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Port – Lers Pass loop from L'Artigue&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 95 km.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on road cycling routes around Auzat?
A: The routes around Auzat offer stunning Pyrenean scenery. You can expect dramatic mountain views, tranquil forests, and picturesque valleys. Notable viewpoints include the panoramic vistas from the summit of Goulier-Neige (1478m) and various points along the challenging mountain passes.

Q: Are there any notable mountain passes to explore by road bike near Auzat?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its mountain passes. Cyclists can tackle iconic climbs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lers Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agnès Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3409330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Latrape Pass&lt;/a&gt;. These passes are integral to many of the challenging routes in the area.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of beautiful water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29446496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang de Soulcem – Soulcem Reservoir loop from Suc-et-Sentenac&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the scenic Soulcem Reservoir. Other nearby lakes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Lake of Comapedrosa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2271161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izourt Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the road conditions like on routes around Auzat?
A: Many routes around Auzat feature mostly well-paved surfaces, providing an enjoyable cycling experience. While generally good, some roads, particularly on less frequented climbs like Goulier-Neige, can be narrower at times, adding to the rustic Pyrenean experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate most about the routes in Auzat?
A: The road cycling routes in Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging climbs, the wild and majestic Pyrenean scenery, and the diverse network of routes that cater to various ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Auzat?
A: The best time for road cycling in Auzat is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During this period, mountain passes are generally clear of snow, and the weather is more stable, offering ideal conditions to enjoy the challenging climbs and stunning landscapes.
tips: With a good 800 meters difference in altitude from Vicdessos, the Port de Lers is not a giant among the Pyrenees passes, but is still very pleasant to drive thanks to low traffic and a very nice road. Initially following the valley bottom, the route climbs rather moderately, leads through very beautiful old oak stands and then in the upper part over some serpentines, but still not excessively steep with the 8% to the open pass.
Lers pond is located in the Ariège Pyrenees, at 1,264 meters above sea level. It lies between Aulus-les-Bains to the southwest, Vicdessos to the southeast and Massat to the northwest.
